standard: Đến trời còn vậy thì nói gì thầy với sếp.
A: Even the heavens are like that, so what can you expect from the teacher or the boss?
B: If even the weather is like this, then what can be said about teachers and bosses.

standard: mê thật cái sắc đỏ của lon nhé.

B: I really love the red color of the can.
C: I really love the red color of that can.

standard: Hôm trước tổ chức kế bên mà chưa có vào xem luôn.
A: The event was held next door the other day, but I didn't get a chance to go see it.
B: The other day it was organized right next door, but I didn't even go in to see it.
C: The other day there was an event next door, but I haven't even gone in to check it out.

C: That's right, in 2010, I was riding a motorcycle when water knocked me down and I rolled around, getting soaking wet from head to toe.

B: This ring looks familiar, doesn't it look like Niem Pham's?

B: What is this woman selling?
C: What does this person sell?

standard: Mãi đẳng cấp chị ơi. Không xạo không nịnh. Nể nhất chị là người sống thẳng.
A: You're always so classy, ​​sister. No pretense, no flattery. What I admire most about you is that you're a straightforward person.
B: Always top-tier, sister. No lying, no flattery. What I admire most about you is that you live honestly.
C: Always so classy, sis. No BS, no sucking up. What I respect most about you is that you're a straightforward person.

standard: Nạ nhỉ? có cả môn phái này nữa thì nhanh giàu nắm.
A: Wow, right? If there's a martial arts school like this, you'll get rich quick.
B: Right? If there's even a sect like this, one would get rich very quickly.
C: Sign up, right? With this whole tier included too, you'd get rich quick, you know.

standard: Tao với mày lọt được này.
A: You and I made it through.
B: You and I managed to get into this.
C: You and I can fit through this.

standard: Mê bé ở giữa.
A: I love the baby in the middle.
B: I love the little one in the middle.
C: I'm obsessed with the one in the middle.

standard: làm tôi phải gọi điện mẹ xem thật không.
A: It made me call my mom to see if it was true.
B: It made me have to call my mother to see if it was true.
C: This makes me have to call mom to check if it's true.

B: Why do you sound so depressed?
C: Why does it sound so bored with life like that?

standard: Không qua cầu sao chạy xuống mương hay vậy?
A: Why did you run into the ditch instead of crossing the bridge?
B: How did you end up in the ditch without even crossing the bridge?
C: Why not cross the bridge instead of running into the ditch?

standard: Gì vậy nhỉ , khổ thật.
A: What's going on? This is terrible.
B: What is this, how miserable.
C: What's this, I wonder? Life is really tough.

standard: Bé Na đó cắn chắc không đau bằng bé này đâu bạn nhỉ?
A: That bite from little Na probably didn't hurt as much as this one, right?
B: That baby Na's bite probably isn't as painful as this baby's, right?
C: That Na person probably doesn't bite as painfully as this one does, right?

standard: Quán này mày có biết quán nào không?
A: Do you know which restaurant this is?
B: Do you know any shops like this one?
C: Do you know any place like this?

standard: Em này nổi bên tiktok nữa ấy chú. Cũng kiếm ra tiền giỏi.
A: This girl is also famous on TikTok, uncle. She's also good at making money.
B: This person is also famous on TikTok, uncle. They are also good at making money.
C: This person is blowing up on TikTok too, you know. They're pretty good at making money too.

standard: thương quá mẹ ạ, không khổ nào bằng.
A: Oh, Mom, I feel so sorry for you. There's no suffering like this.
B: I feel so much pity, Mom; there is no suffering worse than this.
C: I love you so much, Mom. No suffering compares to this.

standard: Ở Triệu Phong cũng giống vậy
A: It's the same in Trieu Phong.
B: It's the same in Trieu Phong.
C: It's the same in Triệu Phong.

standard: Mày thề là mày không chỉnh tí màu nào đi.
A: Swear you didn't adjust the colors at all.
B: Swear that you didn't adjust the colors at all.
C: Swear that you didn't adjust any colors at all.

A: Let's give it our all, guys!
B: Let's give it our all, you guys.
C: We'll go all out for sure, you guys!

standard: Cái bánh này mà ném vào mặt nyc chắc ở Hà Nội cũng nghe thấy nhỉ?
A: If this cake were thrown in my ex's face, I bet people in Hanoi would hear about it, right?
B: If I threw this cake at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it, wouldn't they?
C: This cake, if I threw it at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it too, huh?

standard: Vậy là may mắn rồi, người không bị gì là tốt quá.
A: That's lucky; it's great that no one was hurt.
B: That's lucky then; it's great that the person wasn't hurt.
C: So we're lucky, it's great that the person wasn't hurt.

A: Summer vacation is not boring yet!
B: I'm not tired of summer break yet!
C: Summer vacation hasn't gotten boring yet!

standard: Lúc nhỏ thích mưa, giờ thấy chán.
A: I loved the rain when I was a child, but now I'm tired of it.
B: I liked the rain when I was a child, but now I find it boring.
C: I used to like rain when I was young, but now I find it boring.

standard: Để khi nào nhé .
A: Let's do it sometime.
B: Let's do it some other time.
C: Let's do it sometime.

standard: mà nhìn chiến vậy thì không phải tao đâu
A: But judging by the way that's the fight, it's definitely not me.
B: but looking that fierce, it couldn't be me
C: But looking at how they fight like that, it's definitely not me.

A: I ate it a few days ago, and it was really delicious.
B: I just ate there a few days ago, and I have to admit it was really delicious.
C: I just ate it a few days ago, and it was really delicious indeed.

A: Mai, shouldn't we have a ceremony to officially join the city?
B: We're doing the city joining ceremony tomorrow, right?
C: We're having the city joining ceremony tomorrow, right?

C: That guy's really bold, filming and taking photos without checking the date.

A: Go ahead and run, let's see if your legs are as long as mine.
B: Run, what else? Let's see if your legs are as long as mine.
C: Well then, just go run. Let's see if your legs are even long enough

standard: Hà Tĩnh quê tôi là vậy đấy. Chúc anh nhiều sức khỏe.
A: That's my hometown, Ha Tinh. I wish you good health.
B: That's how my hometown of Ha Tinh is. Wishing you lots of health.
C: That's how my hometown Hà Tĩnh is. Wishing you good health.

A: Wow, that's impressive! Much better than those thugs near my house. They just eat and do nothing, and yet they're always hanging around outside.

C: Impressive, aren't they? Better than those idiots near my house already. They just eat and don't know what else to do, but they sure like to go strutting around outside.

standard: không cần đi đâu cho xa, đây rồi này.
A: No need to go far, it's right here.
B: No need to go anywhere far, it's right here.
C: No need to go anywhere far, it's right here.

A: Going to Hue on a rainy day is incredibly boring, not relaxing at all!
B: Going to Hue and hitting a rainy day is dreadfully boring; how is that supposed to be chill?
C: Going to Hue and hitting a rainy day makes it unbearably boring, there's no chill at all.

B: You're running ads so aggressively; today will probably be a warm day.
C: You run ads that much, today it's probably warm already.

B: In Soc Trang, I call it cốm ống, you guys.

A: Why are you calling me all the time?

A: E, you guys aren't trying to wake me up from the dead.
B: Don't wake me up from the dead, you guys.
C: Don't wake me up, you guys.

A: But back then it tasted so good, Grandma! We'd fight over it when we bought it at the market.

A: It's also Thanh Chuong, but this Thanh Chuong is amazing.
B: It's also Thanh Chương, but this Thanh Chương is truly amazing.

standard: đang chụp hình hay sao mà không thấy nhỉ?
A: Are they taking pictures or something? I can't see them.

A: This time, the insurance company paid the full amount, didn't they?
B: The insurance payout this time is in full, right?

A: Do I have to wear makeup to school every day now?

A: This is my favorite part; I still feel happy remembering it.

standard: Đấy là tôi còn cắt bớt rồi đấy chứ.
A: And that's even after I've already cut it short.
B: And that's after I've already cut some of it out.
C: That's mine, and I've already had to cut it down, you know.

standard: nhớ năm nào nhỉ?
A: What year was it?

C: Which year do you remember?

standard: Dân Huế này đi Đại Nội chưa nhỉ?
A: Have any of you people from Hue ever been to the Imperial Citadel?
B: Has this Hue local visited the Imperial City yet?
C: Have I (we Hue locals) been to the Imperial City yet?

standard: Tao lạy mày, bão Trà Mi ạ.
A: I beg you, Typhoon Tra Mi.
B: I beg you, Storm Trami.
C: I bow to you, Typhoon Trà Mi.

standard: Sáng chúng tôi qua đoạn này quay cho xem đấy.
A: We filmed this section this morning for you to see.
B: This morning we went to this section to film it for you to see.
C: This morning we went past this section to film it for you to see.

standard: Bà có về không mà bà hay bàn quá vây.
A: Is she even coming home? She talks so much about it.
B: Are you even coming back, or why are you talking so much?
C: Are you going home or not, that you're always commenting too much?

A: Did you even watch that play before making such a judgment like a know-it-all?
B: Did you even watch that play before judging like an expert, sister?
C: Did you even watch that play, yet you're judging like an expert?

standard: Bay cứ làm màu thế, chứ làm được gì.
A: Bay just puts on a show, but he can't actually do anything.
B: You just keep putting on a show, but you can't actually do anything.
C: You're always putting on airs like that, but what have you actually accomplished?

standard: Trời mưa tối om mà sao bạn chỉnh gì sáng thế.

B: It's raining and pitch black, so why did you edit it to be so bright?
C: It's raining and pitch dark outside, yet you're so cheerful.

A: If that's the case, then I advise you to do this.

standard: chờ được chắc răng cũng mòn hết rồi
A: Waiting this long would probably wear down all my teeth.
B: By the time I'm done waiting, my teeth will probably have worn away.

standard: Quê chúng tôi thừa rồi. Bão Trà Mi không cần vào nữa đâu.
A: Our hometown already has enough. Typhoon Tra Mi doesn't need to come here anymore.

standard: Mưa lụt thế này chợ không bán lấy đâu ra dưa nhỉ?
A: With all this flooding, the market can't sell anything, so where would the watermelons come from?
B: With flooding like this, the market isn't open, so where can I find any melons?

standard: Nhanh làm gì mà khổ vậy không biết
A: Why rush when you can do it so badly?
B: I don't know why I'm rushing so much just to make things hard for myself.

standard: Không cho đá sân chợ Trụ đâu nhỉ?
A: They won't let us play football at Tru Market, will they?
B: They won't let us play football at the Tru market pitch, right?

standard: Bà giỏi quá. Nhìn một cái là làm được liền, các cháu thì đi học cả tháng.
A: She's so capable. She can do it immediately just by looking at it, while the children are away at school for a whole month.

C: Grandma is so amazing. She just has to look at something once and she can do it right away, while the grandchildren take a whole month of school.

standard: Nôn Tết để được về quê

B: Longing for Tet so I can return to my hometown

standard: Nghĩ lại thời đó đã thật, thích đi đâu là gọi cái đi luôn.
